_meta.title: Anycubic (Kobra) — MQTT topic grammar + message envelope

derived_from.section: Transport & connection — Topic grammar / Message envelope
derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from Anycubic's own SlicerNext (OrcaSlicer fork) device layer + sanitized owner MQTT captures. No code copied; no cert/key/credential values included.

notes: Values shown are placeholders. `{deviceId}` is device-specific and must be sanitized in any captured example.
The two Anycubic dialects (avata=Kobra X, gkapi=Kobra 2/3/S1) share this topic grammar; only a few report fields differ.

Feeders: `multiColorBox` is the ACE bank-of-slots; `extfilbox` is the single external spool (command verb `extfilbox:setInfo`; reports a `loaded`/runout flag). Both are documented in the paper's Multi-material / feeders section.

segments.name: msgType
segments.note: The MESSAGE/REPORT type — NOT the action verb. The action lives in the payload (see rules).

subscribe_patterns.purpose: Wildcard subscribe for all printer reports.

envelope.description: Compact JSON payload. The verb is `type`+`action` INSIDE the payload — never appended to the topic.

action.note: Verb, e.g. 'start', 'set', 'setSpeed'. THIS is the command; it is not in the topic.

correlation.primary: Echoed `msgid` on a `.../response` topic (a bare `{"msgid": ...}` ACK).
correlation.fallback: For getInfo-style replies, a report-topic suffix match.

rules.text: A command's action goes in the JSON payload (`type`+`action`), NEVER in the topic. Appending the action to the topic makes the printer silently drop it ('messageHandler not found') while STILL ACKing — it looks like it worked but did nothing.
Wire spelling is load-bearing: the temperature verb is `tempature` (misspelled) and the fan verb is `fan:setSpeed` (not `fan:set`).
The final topic segment `msgType` is the report/message type, not the action — the same command's action is carried only in the payload.
